meaning: vt.椹冲洖,鍚﹀喅,鏀厤,鍒舵湇
examples: It is theoretically possible for him to overrule their decision, but highly unlikely.
If his generals disagree, he will overrule them.
As a referee, you must also be confident and alert enough to overrule your assistant when necessary.
Her father, therefore, is in a position to overrule his daughter at any time.
Often the EPA oversteps its statutory authority so blatantly that federal judges frequently overrule EPA actions.
